---
title: "Home"
---

## Senior Biostatistician

I am a biostatistician with over five years of experience supporting clinical, epidemiologic, and public health research. I focus on clear study design, rigorous statistical modeling, and reproducible analytic workflows to support reliable scientific conclusions.

I work closely with clinicians and public health researchers to translate complex data into interpretable results that inform research, program evaluation, and evidence-based practice.

## Research Background
HIV and infectious diseases, cardiovascular disease, neurology, and metabolic disorders (including diabetes)

## Areas of Expertise

Applied biostatistics across clinical and public health research, including study design, longitudinal and survival analysis, and causal inference methods.

- Regression modeling (GLM, GLMM)
- Longitudinal data analysis
- Survival analysis
- Causal inference using inverse probability of treatment weighting (IPTW)
- Study design and power/sample size calculations
- Penalized regression (LASSO)
- Multiple imputation
- Neighborhood- and multilevel data analysis

## Tools
R, SAS, Python, Quarto, Git, R Shiny, Markdown
